content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer matrix AAA of NNN rows and MMM columns, and an integer XXX.

A **submatrix** is defined as a rectangle of values inside AAA. More formally, given two intervals [l,r][l, r][l,r] and [L,R][L, R][L,R], the corresponding submatrix consists of the values aija_{ij}aij​ such that l≤i≤rl\le i\le rl≤i≤r and L≤j≤RL\le j\le RL≤j≤R. Two submatrices are considered different if one of the ranges [l,r][l,r][l,r] or [L,R][L,R][L,R] differs.

Chef uses f(X)f(X)f(X) to denote the total number of submatrices BBB in which max⁡(B)⊕min⁡(B)=X,\max(B) \oplus \min(B)=X,max(B)⊕min(B)=X,

where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR, max⁡(B)\max(B)max(B) denotes the maximum integer in BBB, and min⁡(B)\min(B)min(B) denotes the minimum integer in BBB.

Chef does not know in which order he would like to put the rows. He wishes to know the value of f(X)f(X)f(X) for every permutation of rows, but since the amount of them can be too large, he wants to know the sum of the answers over all permutations.

###Input


The first line contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and XXX.
Next NNN lines contains MMM integers each: the jjj-th integer of the iii-th line AijA_{ij}Aij​.
###Output

Output the sum of f(X)f(X)f(X) over all permutations of the rows of AAA.

###Constraints


1≤N≤81 \leq N \leq  81≤N≤8
1≤M≤1051 \leq M \leq 10^51≤M≤105
0≤X,Aij≤1090 \leq X, A_{ij} \leq 10^90≤X,Aij​≤109
